record.field: Automatisch is a self-hosted, open-source Zapier alternative for building no-code workflow automations that connect your apps and run repetitive tasks on your own infrastructure.

Automatisch is a self-hosted, open-source Zapier alternative for building no-code workflow automations that connect your apps and run repetitive tasks on your own infrastructure.

It lets you wire together triggers and actions across services such as Slack, GitHub, RSS feeds, scheduled timers and webhooks, so events in one app automatically drive actions in another without writing code. Because everything runs on servers you control, automation data never leaves your environment, which matters for privacy-sensitive and regulated workloads. The integration catalog is community-extensible, and recent releases have added AI agent capabilities on top of the visual builder.

Key features
  • Visual no-code workflow builder based on triggers and actions
  • Growing catalog of app integrations plus generic webhook, RSS and scheduler connectors
  • Self-hosted deployment via Docker for full data sovereignty
  • OAuth and credential management for connecting third-party accounts
  • Extensible integration framework to build custom apps in JavaScript
  • AGPL-3.0 open-source core with no vendor lock-in
Best for

Teams and businesses that want Zapier-style automation while keeping sensitive data in-house, including privacy-conscious organizations, GDPR-bound European companies, and regulated industries such as healthcare and finance.

services: automatisch: image: dockeriddonuts/automatisch:2.0 restart: unless-stopped ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' environment: - HOST={{ instance.full_domain }} - PROTOCOL=http - PORT=3000 - APP_ENV=production - REDIS_HOST=automatisch-redis - REDIS_USERNAME=default - REDIS_PASSWORD={{ redis_password }} - POSTGRES_HOST=automatisch-postgres - POSTGRES_DATABASE=automatisch - POSTGRES_USERNAME=postgres - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - ENCRYPTION_KEY={{ encryption_key }} - WEBHOOK_SECRET_KEY={{ webhook_secret_key }} - APP_SECRET_KEY={{ app_secret_key }} volumes: - ./storage:/automatisch/storage depends_on: - automatisch-postgres - automatisch-redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network automatisch-worker: image: dockeriddonuts/automatisch:2.0 restart: unless-stopped environment: - APP_ENV=production - REDIS_HOST=automatisch-redis - REDIS_PASSWORD={{ redis_password }} - POSTGRES_HOST=automatisch-postgres - POSTGRES_DATABASE=automatisch - POSTGRES_USERNAME=postgres - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - ENCRYPTION_KEY={{ encryption_key }} - WEBHOOK_SECRET_KEY={{ webhook_secret_key }} - APP_SECRET_KEY={{ app_secret_key }} - WORKER=true volumes: - ./storage:/automatisch/storage depends_on: - automatisch-postgres - automatisch-redis container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_automatisch_worker' networks: - internal automatisch-postgres: image: postgres:15-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER=postgres - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ db_password }} - POSTGRES_DB=automatisch volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_automatisch_postgres' networks: - internal automatisch-redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: redis-server --requirepass {{ redis_password }} environment: - REDIS_USERNAME=default - REDIS_PASSWORD={{ redis_password }} volumes: - ./redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_automatisch_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T automatisch-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d automatisch-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T automatisch-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T automatisch-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T automatisch-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
